original_hypothesis.title: Reconfiguration can brick locking/unlocking by setting critical dependency addresses to zero or incompatible contracts
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: configUpdated() re-wires accountManager/migrationManager/snuggeryManager/nftOverlord from configStorage without validation. If configStorage returns address(0) or an incompatible contract, _lock and unlock can revert (e.g., accountManager.getPlayer/forceHarvest calls). This can freeze deposits/withdrawals and break NFT reveal accounting during lockdrop.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: While onlyConfigStorage gates configUpdated, the operational blast radius of a bad config push is large. This is a common production incident class (misconfiguration) and can be triggered by compromised configStorage governance as well.
deployment_assumptions: ConfigStorage can be updated post-deploy and may point to new addresses during upgrades/maintenance
No on-chain validation exists elsewhere to prevent setting zero or wrong-interface addresses
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: No deployment evidence provided showing configStorage immutability or validation of StorageKey addresses; LockManager.configUpdated() performs unchecked assignments from configStorage.getAddress(...)
preconditions: ConfigStorage (or its admin/governance) updates StorageKey.* addresses to zero or incorrect contract
Users attempt to lock/unlock (calls to accountManager/nftOverlord are executed)

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Mutable wiring without runtime validation makes critical paths sensitive to config mistakes
initial_operational_evidence.admin_dependency: High; correctness relies on configStorage integrity and update procedure
initial_operational_evidence.exploitation_window: During upgrades, migrations, emergency config changes

operational_questions: Who controls configStorage and what is the change management process?
Are there on-chain invariants enforced in configStorage (nonzero, contract codehash allowlist)?
Is there a rollback mechanism if configUpdated bricks locking?
validation_requirements: Inspect IConfigStorage implementation for access control and validation
Simulate setting AccountManager to address(0) and observe lock/unlock behavior

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Push a config update in configStorage for one dependency
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: Call LockManager.configUpdated() (or rely on automated trigger)
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: User operations revert due to bad external call targets

potential_operational_impact.administrative_compromise: Indirect control over protocol behavior via dependency substitution
potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Protocol-wide deposit/withdraw halt; lockdrop NFT accounting failure
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: Medium (depends on ability to correct config quickly)
potential_operational_impact.affected_operations: All user lock/unlock flows and lockdrop mint notifications

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: ConfigStorage updates followed by sudden revert spikes in lock/unlock

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
